step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{C}
**Clinical Reasoning:** The provided evidence repeatedly refers to "biofilm of various bacteria" and specifically identifies *plaque* as the biofilm formed on tooth surfaces composed mainly of streptococci and anaerobes. Multiple sources explicitly describe plaque formation, its components, and its link to caries through acid production. While enamel (option A) and dental caries (option B) are related elements, neither defines the biofilm itself. Saliva (option D) is a component of the oral environment but not the biofilm. Only option C—dental plaque—is directly defined as the biofilm on tooth surfaces in the text.
